findings.description: Three distinct voice attributes (confident-not-boastful, warm-not-overly-emotional, clear-not-complicated), each with its own positive/negative pairing and rationale, are collapsed into a single rule. This loses the individual contrast pairs and the specific guidance for each trait (e.g. 'never overstated or sensationalized' vs 'plain, respectful language').

The unit stating 'Tone may flex slightly by context; core voice never changes' is not represented by any rule. This is a distinct governance statement about permissible flexibility (soft/contextual) alongside an invariant core (hard/must-not-change), and its omission means the group misses a key scope-defining rule that qualifies all three voice traits.

payload.description: Core brand voice must never change even though tone may flex slightly by context.

findings.description: Hardness is labeled soft_guidance for all three merged traits, but the source uses direct prescriptive contrasts ('never overstated or sensationalized') suggesting at least the negative constraints (never/not) carry harder, near-mandatory polarity rather than uniform soft guidance.

Rule lacks explicit scope/applicability facets (e.g. which content types/channels the Brand Voice section governs) and no owner/exception governance is captured, despite this being a heading-scoped compliance section.
